title: Apple Vision Pro exec is reportedly leaving for OpenAI
summary: Paul Meade, Apple's VP of Vision Pro, is leaving the company to join OpenAI's hardware team. This departure signals talent migration from Apple's AR/VR efforts to OpenAI's expanding hardware initiatives.
content: Paul Meade, the Apple vice president in charge of the Vision Pro headset, is reportedly leaving the company to join OpenAI’s hardware team.

zh.title: Apple Vision Pro高管据报离职加入OpenAI
zh.summary: Apple Vision Pro副总裁Paul Meade正在离开公司加入OpenAI的硬件团队。这一离职标志着人才从Apple的AR/VR努力向OpenAI不断扩展的硬件计划迁移。

title: The Guardian’s Kai Wright refuses to buy a new phone
summary: Peabody Award-winning journalist Kai Wright, co-host of The Guardian's Stateside podcast, has cultivated a reputation for steadfastly refusing to buy new phones. The profile explores his daily routine and philosophy toward technology in an age of constant upgrades.
content:
That is an objectively dope couch. | Image: Kai Wright	

Kai Wright is the co-host of Stateside with Kai and Carter over at the Guardian. But Wright has been bringing his unique insights to listeners for years. He's also hosted Notes From America, The United States of Anxiety, and Indivisible. He's a Peabody Award-winning journalist who has profiled powerful men, explored what it means to be American, and chronicled the AIDS epidemic.
When he's not diving deep on sex, race, and politics, he's gardening, listening to John Coltrane, and steadfastly refusing to buy a new phone. Wright was kind enough to give us a glimpse into his daily routine, how he unwinds, and offer some simple but powerful advi …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: The Guardian记者Kai Wright拒绝购买新手机
zh.summary: 获得Peabody奖的记者Kai Wright是The Guardian的Stateside播客联合主持人，他因坚决拒绝购买新手机而声名远播。这个档案探讨了他在不断升级时代对科技的日常习惯和哲学。

title: The fittest founder in the room got cancer. Here’s how he used AI to fight back.
summary: Connor Christou, a fitness-focused founder diagnosed with cancer, fed his health data—blood results, scans, wearables, and journal entries—into Claude AI to analyze and inform his treatment strategy. The case demonstrates how AI can assist patients with personalized data analysis during health crises.
content: When confronted with cancer, Connor Christou fed everything tied tied to his regime — blood results, scan data, wearable output, journal entries — into Claude.

zh.title: 最健康的创始人患了癌症。他如何用AI反击
zh.summary: 健身爱好者创始人Connor Christou被诊断出患有癌症，他将健康数据——血液检查结果、扫描、可穿戴设备和日记——输入Claude AI以分析并指导他的治疗策略。这个案例展示了AI在健康危机期间如何帮助患者进行个性化数据分析。

title: Indie developers got tired of waiting for a new Star Fox, so they’re making their own
summary: With Nintendo dormant on the Star Fox franchise since Star Fox Zero on Wii U, indie developers have created their own Star Fox-inspired games to fill the void. The indie efforts underscore nostalgia's power and the opportunity gap left by major franchises lacking new official entries.
content:
Nostalgia remains a powerful force. So much so that, in exploring the echoes of a late-'90s childhood spent skimming the water of Corneria and sneering "cocky little freaks!" in time with a monkey encased in a Gundam suit, I'm simultaneously describing playing Star Fox 64 (Lylat Wars if you're nasty) in 1997 and streaming it through Nintendo Switch Online today.
The franchise has been revived through a splashy remake on the Switch 2, but it's also a series that has not seen an all-new entry since Star Fox Zero on the Wii U. Yet Nintendo's neglect of the series has been gently offset by indie creators. Ex-Zodiac and Whisker Squadron: Survivo …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 独立游戏开发者厌倦了等待,决定自己制作星际火狐游戏
zh.summary: 由于任天堂自Wii U的《星际火狐零》之后一直没有推出新作,独立游戏开发者创建了自己的星际火狐启发游戏来填补空白。这些独立开发工作突显了怀旧情感的力量以及大型游戏特许经营权缺乏新官方作品所留下的机会空隙。

title: Why is Apple asking me to pay more for Big Tech’s AI obsession?
summary: Apple has raised prices across multiple products—MacBook Pro ($300), iPad Air ($150), HomePod Mini ($30)—with CEO Tim Cook citing AI hardware demands as unavoidable costs. The company joins other tech firms in passing along AI-driven expense increases to consumers.
content:
Tim Cook recently said price increases were "unavoidable" and described the company's pricing as "unsustainable." The 16-inch MacBook Pro saw its price go up by $300. The 11-inch iPad Air went from $599 to $749. Even the HomePod Mini got a $30 bump to $129. Cook squarely placed the blame at the feet of the AI industry, which is not surprising. RAMageddon has already come for your desktop PCs and gaming consoles. The Xbox has seen its price climb nearly 25 percent depending on the model, and Nothing even canceled an entire phone launch. Apple is just the most recent to jack up prices and point the finger at AI.
The price hikes are "basic eco …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 为什么苹果要求我为科技巨头的人工智能狂热买单?
zh.summary: 苹果公司提高了多种产品的价格——MacBook Pro提价300美元、iPad Air提价150美元、HomePod Mini提价30美元——CEO蒂姆·库克将人工智能硬件需求列为不可避免的成本。该公司与其他科技公司一样,将人工智能驱动的费用增加转嫁给消费者。

title: Inside the room where the smart home industry is still betting on Matter
summary: Four years after its Amsterdam launch, the Matter smart home interoperability standard—backed by Apple, Google, Amazon, and Samsung—continues gaining industry support despite slow real-world adoption. The collaborative protocol aims to eliminate ecosystem lock-in and simplify cross-brand device setup.
content:
Four years ago, overlooking a canal in Amsterdam, the smart home industry collectively launched Matter, the one interoperability standard to rule them all. Heralded as the solution to the industry's struggles, Matter was built on open standards and existing technologies and is the result of years of collaboration between traditional rivals, including Apple, Google, Amazon, and Samsung.
Matter promised an end to walled gardens and ecosystem lock-in. It promised to make a smart home device, like a lock, lightbulb, or sensor, easy to buy and set up. It promised you could choose any brand, use any platform, no expertise required - it would just …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 在智能家居行业仍在押注Matter标准的会议室内
zh.summary: 在荷兰阿姆斯特丹推出四年后,由苹果、谷歌、亚马逊和三星支持的Matter智能家居互操作性标准继续获得行业支持,尽管现实应用仍然缓慢。这一协作协议的目标是消除生态系统锁定并简化跨品牌设备设置。

title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like  models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on
summary: Asian AI startups are launching models with capabilities comparable to Anthropic's Mythos to circumvent U.S. export restrictions. This represents a significant loss of overseas market opportunity for American AI laboratories as international competitors fill the void created by export controls.
content: New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban. U.S. AI labs may never recover this enormous market.

zh.title: 亚洲人工智能初创公司推出类似Mythos的模型，应对Anthropic的出口禁令
zh.summary: 亚洲人工智能初创公司正在推出功能与Anthropic的Mythos相当的模型，以规避美国出口限制。这代表美国人工智能实验室失去了海外市场机会，因为国际竞争对手正在填补出口管制造成的空白。

title: This puzzle game’s simple premise hides surprising depth
summary: What's the Password is a puzzle game with over 100 challenges centered on entering four-digit codes. Despite its simple constraint, the game demonstrates surprising depth through cleverly varied puzzle formats and clues that maintain novelty.
content:
What's the Password? has a simple concept: To solve each of the game's more than 100 puzzles, you have to type in the right four-digit password on a number pad. That might sound like a limited constraint. But the simplicity gives solo developer Dan DiIorio, better known as TrampolineTales, lots of room to play with clever ideas. Over the course of a few hours, the game never stopped surprising me.
Puzzle clues come in several different formats. Some are written text; the very first puzzle is a sentence that tells you what numbers to punch in. But you'll also have to decipher four-digit codes from things like the blinking digits of a clock,  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 这款益智游戏简单的前提隐藏着惊人的深度
zh.summary: "What's the Password"是一款包含100多个挑战的益智游戏，围绕输入四位数代码展开。尽管约束简单，该游戏通过精巧变化的谜题格式和保持新颖性的线索展现了惊人的深度。

title: This might be the new best smart speaker
summary: The Verge's Installer column highlights a new smart speaker as a top consumer tech pick. The article provides limited detail about the device's specific capabilities in the available excerpt.
content:
Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 134, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, hope you're okay in all this heat, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) 
This week, I've been reading about Polymarket lies and Jalen Brunson and the Arts and Crafts Movement, watching Not Suitable for Work and way too much of the World Cup, trying to catch up on The Bear before the final season starts, playing a bunch of Hank Green's 4×3 game, trying my absolute hardest not to preorder this amazing Stanley Kubrick collection even though I'm obviously going to, buying a bunch of …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 这可能是新的最佳智能扬声器
zh.summary: The Verge的Installer专栏将一款新的智能扬声器列为顶级消费者科技选择。文章在可用的摘录中对设备的具体功能提供的细节有限。

title: Apple and Audi alumni have made a luxe EV based on the moon buggy
summary: Former Apple and Audi designers have created the Amble One, a $25,000 street-legal electric buggy designed for luxury resort clientele. The vehicle evokes the iconic moon buggy aesthetic while targeting the high-end hospitality market.
content: The Amble One is a street-legal $25,000 electric buggy designed for luxury resorts.

zh.title: 苹果和奥迪校友推出月球车风格的豪华电动车
zh.summary: 苹果和奥迪前设计师推出Amble One，一款25,000美元的街道合法电动车，专为豪华度假村设计。该车采用标志性月球车美学风格，瞄准高端酒店市场。

title: 24 Prime Day deals Verge readers are grabbing before Prime Day ends
summary: Verge readers are trending multiple Prime Day deals across categories, including the compact Xteink X4 e-reader, Hoto Electric Screwdriver, and Birdbuddy's camera-equipped bird feeder. The data reveals which discounted tech and home products most appealed to tech-savvy shoppers during the sale.
content:
The tiny Xteink X4 reader is proving surprisingly popular.	

There are an overwhelming number of Prime Day deals to sort through, which is why we spend so much time highlighting products we’ve already tested and can stand behind. But our recommendations are only one way to identify a worthwhile deal: another is seeing which products fellow Verge readers actually buy.
Below, we’ve rounded up the deals that have resonated most with readers who spend as much time thinking about tech as we do. It’s a great way to see what people actually think is a great deal — and what products everyone has been waiting for a price drop on.
Some of the favorites are exactly what you’d expect, like AirPods and Kindles. Others, like Birdbuddy’s camera-equipped smart bird feeder, were a more surprising. If you’re looking for some shopping inspiration, or are simply curious what your fellow readers have been adding to their carts, here are the products that have proven most popular so far during Prime Day.
Hoto 3.6V Electric Screwdriver Kit Pro
Hoto’s rechargeable screwdriver is ideal for small repairs. It offers three torque modes, a rotational speed of up to 220RPM, and a helpful circular LED to illuminate dark spaces. The durable case also holds all 25 steel bits and an extension bar for getting into hard-to-reach places.

Where to Buy:

 $49.99 $28.49 at Amazon
 $49.99 $28.49 at Walmart

Birdbuddy Pro (solar)
Birdbuddy’s charming, camera-equipped feeder identifies and snaps shots of each bird that stops by, supplementing its 5-megapixel stills with insightful facts via an app. The solar roof helps keep the camera’s battery topped up even when it’s cloudy outside.

Where to Buy:

 $299 $168 at Amazon
 $299 $239 at Birdbuddy

Xteink X4 e-reader
The Xteink X4 is significantly smaller and more pocketable than any of the Kindle and Kobo models currently available, and feature magnetic mounts so they can be stuck to the back of MagSafe-compatible smartphones. The bundled software is a little

zh.title: Verge读者在Prime Day结束前购买的24个Prime Day优惠
zh.summary: Verge读者在多个类别中关注多个Prime Day优惠,包括紧凑型Xteink X4电子阅读器、浩拓电动螺丝刀和Birdbuddy配备摄像头的喂鸟器。数据显示了哪些折扣科技和家居产品最受科技爱好者在销售期间的欢迎。

title: Trump Admin releases Anthropic  Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
summary: The Trump administration has authorized over 100 US companies and government agencies to access Anthropic's Mythos AI model, including access for non-American employees. The authorization represents significant government backing for the AI system's deployment across public and private sectors.
content: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.

zh.title: 特朗普政府发布Anthropic Mythos供100多家美国公司和机构使用
zh.summary: 特朗普政府已授权100多家美国公司和政府机构访问Anthropic的Mythos AI模型,包括为非美国员工提供访问权限。此授权代表政府对该AI系统在公共和私人部门部署的重大支持。

title: FTC gives Musk the OK to acquire SpaceX alumni startup Mesh
summary: FTC approved Musk's acquisition of Mesh, a SpaceX alumni startup that launched with $50 million Series A funding in February. This signals regulatory approval for the deal.
content: Mesh came out of stealth in February with a $50 million Series A.

zh.title: FTC批准马斯克收购SpaceX校友初创公司Mesh
zh.summary: 美国联邦贸易委员会批准了马斯克对Mesh的收购。Mesh是一家SpaceX校友创业公司，于2月获得5000万美元A轮融资。此次批准表示对该交易的监管支持。

title: Anthropic’s Mythos 5 is back
summary: After a two-week negotiation with the Trump administration, Anthropic's Mythos 5 model has been approved for limited deployment to select organizations, though the public-facing Fable 5 remains unapproved with no clear timeline for release.
content:
After a rollercoaster negotiation process with the Trump administration that dragged on for two weeks, Anthropic's Mythos 5 is finally back in action - at least, somewhat, for a select group of organizations, according to a letter from the government to Anthropic that was viewed by The Verge. Fable 5, however - the public-facing Mythos-class model - appears to still be in limbo, with no apparent timeline for a rollout agreement.
The letter, dated June 26th and sent by Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick to Anthropic co-founder Tom Brown, who had been recently leading negotiations, states that there's been a "revision to the license requiremen …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Anthropic的Mythos 5模型回归
zh.summary: 经过与特朗普政府两周的谈判后，Anthropic的Mythos 5模型已被批准有限部署到特定组织。但是，面向公众的Fable 5仍未获批，暂无发布时间表。
